paper_title: Honeybee Pesticide Analysis and Toxicity Evaluation

methods.description: Collection and preparation of honeybee samples for pesticide analysis, including homogenization and storage procedures.

steps.description: The honeybee samples used in this study were collected at the Bee Research Institute at Dol in the Czech Republic on the 17th-18th of July 2013 from an Apis mellifera carnica colony.
steps.searchable_summary: collect honeybee samples from a specific location and date

steps.description: 1.2 ml of acetonitrile were added to each honeybee in the 2-ml Potter-Elvehjem homogeniser, and the samples were homogenised using a drilling machine.
The homogenate was transferred to 1.5-ml centrifuge tubes and centrifuged at 20,000 × g and 4 °C for 20 min.
The samples were transferred to brown 1.5-ml glass vials with screw caps and stored in a rack at -30 °C until use.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples in glass vials at -30 °C

methods.description: Analysis of honeybee and pollen samples for a wide range of pesticides using LC-MS/MS systems.

steps.description: The quats and 148 pesticides were analysed using direct injection with no sample pre-treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pesticides using direct injection LC-MS/MS

steps.description: The samples used for glyphosate analysis were first derivatised and further concentrated using SPE columns.
steps.searchable_summary: derivatize and concentrate samples for glyphosate analysis

steps.description: Quats were analysed using an LC-MS/MS system according to the Applied Biosystems 114AP43-01 application guide.

methods.description: Evaluation of the toxicity of identified pesticides using available data from various databases.

steps.description: To evaluate the toxicity of the identified pesticides, we searched the available data in the following databases.
steps.searchable_summary: search databases for pesticide toxicity data
